hero.titleLine2: with a focus on long-term remission
hero.subtitle: Personalized therapy and thoughtful, gentle patient care.

about.label: About
about.title: Vitiligo treatment with care and results
about.description: Kasymkhanova Aliya Aidarbekouna is a specialist with 38 years of experience in treating vitiligo and skin pigmentation disorders. A pioneer of phototherapy and surgical vitiligo treatment methods in Kazakhstan.

stats.transplants: years of surgical vitiligo treatment experience
stats.publications: Member of the Asian Society for Pigment Cell Research (ASPCR) since 2026

about.milestone2006: First in Kazakhstan to introduce phototherapy and establish the first Vitiligo Center in Shymkent
about.milestone2010: Defended her PhD thesis on comprehensive vitiligo treatment using narrow-band phototherapy NB UVB 311 nm
about.milestone2015: First in Kazakhstan to introduce surgical treatment methods — over 180 successful transplantations
about.milestone2023: President of World Vitiligo Day 2023 (VR Foundation, USA)
about.milestone2026: World Vitiligo Day 2026 at Vitiligo Clinic — first transplantation performed under general anesthesia. A new stage in the development of surgical vitiligo treatment in Kazakhstan.
about.patent1: Patent RK №2 22737, 12.12.2008 — ‘Method of treating patients with vitiligo’
about.pub2: Chapter in ‘A Step-by-Step Guide to Diagnosis, Treatment and Prophylaxis’ (2017)
about.pub3: Thesis ‘New possibilities for prolonging remission with vitiligo’ — Toronto, 2017
about.pub4: Co-author: Frontiers in Medicine, section Dermatology (2025)
about.pub5: Author of over 50 published scientific works
about.award3: Medal named after Academician M.K. Yegizbayev ‘For Significant Contribution to Healthcare Development’ (2023)
about.mem2: Honorary Member of the Association of Dermatovenereologists and Cosmetologists of the Kyrgyz Republic
about.mem3: Member of the Scientific Advisory Board of the Vitiligo Research Foundation (VR Foundation, USA)
about.mem4: Active participant in international forums in the USA, Canada, Italy, Israel, India, Russia, Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan
